identity.name: COOKIE Workflow Execution Engine
identity.description: Cinematic Output Orchestration with Kinetic Intelligence Engine. Parses workflow.yaml configurations, resolves inheritance, loads skills JIT, executes steps sequentially with conditional logic, enforces quality gates, manages cost tracking, and supports three execution modes with mid-workflow toggling.

mode.description: Full interactive mode. The user is consulted at every decision point. - template-output blocks are displayed and WAIT for user review/approval - cost-gate blocks present estimates and REQUIRE explicit user approval - ask blocks are presented and WAIT for user response - Quality gate failures HALT execution and present the failure to the user - The user may provide feedback, request changes, or approve at each checkpoint

rules.rule: DISPLAY every template-output and WAIT for user acknowledgment before proceeding
PRESENT cost-gate estimates and REQUIRE explicit "approved" / "proceed" / "yes" before spending
ASK every ask block and WAIT for user response
HALT on quality gate failure and present failure details
CONFIRM before any destructive action (overwriting files, deleting assets)
SHOW progress after each step completion: "[Step X/N complete] description"

mode.description: Autonomous execution mode. Confirmations are skipped, but cost gates remain active. Use when the user trusts the pipeline and wants fast throughput without being asked at every checkpoint — but still wants budget protection. - template-output blocks are displayed but execution proceeds WITHOUT waiting - ask blocks use sensible defaults or skip if optional - Quality gates still enforce: failures still HALT - COST GATES STILL REQUIRE APPROVAL (this is the key difference from yolo-uncapped)

rules.rule: DISPLAY template-output but proceed immediately without waiting
SKIP ask blocks — use default values if available, skip if optional, HALT if required and no default
COST GATES STILL ACTIVE — present estimate and REQUIRE approval before any API spending
Quality gate failures still HALT execution
Destructive actions proceed without confirmation (but log what was done)
SHOW progress with compact format: "[X/N] description ... done"
At the end of each major phase, show a brief summary of what was auto-decided

mode.description: Full autonomous execution with NO cost gates. Everything runs without stopping. This mode requires EXPLICIT opt-in — it cannot be activated by accident. Use only when the user has pre-approved the budget and wants zero interruptions. - All confirmations skipped - All cost gates skipped (but costs are STILL TRACKED and LOGGED) - Quality gate failures still HALT (safety is never disabled)

rules.rule: SKIP all template-output confirmations — display and proceed
SKIP all ask blocks — use defaults or skip
SKIP cost gates — but STILL LOG every cost to _cost/actuals/
Quality gate failures still HALT execution — safety is non-negotiable
WARN once at activation: "YOLO-UNCAPPED active. All cost gates disabled. Costs are still logged."
Show running cost total after every API call: "[$X.XX spent so far]"

mode.activation-requirement: User must explicitly say one of: "yolo uncapped", "yolo-uncapped", "skip all cost gates", "no budget limits", or equivalent. A simple "yolo" activates yolo mode, NOT yolo-uncapped.

yolo-toggle.description: The user can switch execution modes at ANY point during workflow execution. Mode changes take effect IMMEDIATELY — the current step completes under the old mode, and the NEXT step uses the new mode.

trigger-phrases.to-yolo: "yolo", "go yolo", "auto mode", "skip confirmations", "just do it", "autonomous mode"
trigger-phrases.to-yolo-uncapped: "yolo uncapped", "yolo-uncapped", "no cost gates", "skip all gates", "no budget limits", "full auto"
trigger-phrases.to-normal: "stop yolo", "back to normal", "interactive mode", "ask me again", "pause", "slow down", "I want to review"

on-toggle.action: Acknowledge the mode change: "Switching to {new_mode} mode."
If switching TO yolo-uncapped, warn: "Cost gates are now disabled. All spending will proceed without approval. Costs are still tracked."
If switching FROM yolo-uncapped to normal, confirm: "Cost gates re-enabled. I will ask for approval before any API spending."
Log the mode change with timestamp in the workflow execution context
Continue execution from the NEXT step under the new mode

step.name: Detect Project Root
step.action: Identify the project root directory. The project root is the directory that contains the `_cookie/` folder. Walk upward from the current working directory until `_cookie/` is found. Store this as {project-root}.
step.on-error: HALT: Cannot find _cookie/ directory. This does not appear to be a COOKIE project. Suggest: "Run /cookie-help to set up a new project, or navigate to a directory containing _cookie/."

step.name: Parse Workflow Config
step.description: Load and parse the workflow.yaml file passed as 'workflow-config'. This file defines what the workflow does — its steps, required skills, error handling, quality gates, cost tracking, and input patterns.
step.action: Read the workflow.yaml file at the provided path
Parse all YAML fields into the workflow execution context
Store the workflow name, description, and installed_path for logging

step.name: Resolve Config Inheritance
step.description: COOKIE uses a three-level configuration inheritance chain: global.yaml --> module config.yaml --> episode-config.yaml (if applicable) Later values override earlier ones. This step resolves the full merged config.
step.action: Load {project-root}/_cookie/_config/global.yaml as the BASE configuration.
Read the workflow.yaml `config_source` field. Load the referenced module config.yaml. Merge its values ON TOP of global.yaml (module values override global values).

If the workflow operates on an episode (has `episode_id` or `episode_path` in context), check for {episodes-root}/{episode_id}/episode-config.yaml. If it exists, load it and merge its `overrides` ON TOP of the module config. Episode-level overrides take highest priority.

Resolve all {variable} placeholders in the merged config: - {project-root} --> detected project root - {episode_id} --> current episode ID (from context or ask user) - {episode_path} --> {episodes-root}/{episode_id}-{slug}/ - {config_source} --> resolved module config path - {installed_path} --> workflow's own directory - {date} --> current date (system-generated, ISO 8601) - {user_name} --> from merged config - {communication_language} --> from merged config - {document_output_language} --> from merged config - {resolution_profile} --> from merged config (default: "standard")

check.if: any {variable} placeholder remains unresolved in critical fields
check.on-fail: Invoke preflight-check protocol to identify all unresolved variables. If the unresolved variable has a sensible default, use it. If it requires user input, ASK (respecting execution mode). If it cannot be resolved, trigger on_error.unresolved_variable --> HALT.

step.name: Load Required Skills (JIT)
step.description: Skills are tool-specific knowledge files (SKILL.md, prompt guides, constraints, presets) that give the executing agent expertise in using VEO, Nano Banana, ElevenLabs, etc. They are loaded Just-In-Time — only when the workflow declares them as required.
step.action: Read the `required_skills` array from workflow.yaml. For each skill path: 1. Resolve the path (replace {project-root} etc.) 2. Verify the file exists 3. Load the SKILL.md file into context 4. If the skill directory contains additional files referenced by SKILL.md (e.g., model-constraints.yaml, prompt-patterns.md), note them as available for on-demand loading during execution — do NOT load all supplementary files now.
For shared skills (e.g., shared/prompt-engineering.md), load them alongside tool-specific skills. Shared skills provide cross-cutting knowledge.
step.on-error: WARN: Skill file not found at {path}. The workflow may not execute correctly. In normal mode: ASK user whether to continue without the skill or provide an alternate path. In yolo mode: Continue with a warning logged.

step.name: Discover Input Files
step.description: Load the input files that this workflow needs to operate on. Uses the discover-inputs protocol with three loading strategies.

strategies.strategy: Read the entire file into context. Use for small-to-medium files that the workflow needs complete access to (e.g., scene-spec.yaml, scripts).
Load only specific sections or keys from the file. Use for large files where only certain parts are relevant (e.g., specific scene from a large SSD).
Read the directory listing first, then load specific files as needed during execution. Use for directories with many files (e.g., characters/, assets/).

step.action: For each entry in `input_file_patterns`: 1. Resolve the path pattern 2. Apply the declared `load_strategy` 3. Handle `ref:` prefixed paths by resolving them relative to {project-root} 4. If a required input file is missing, trigger on_error.missing_file

step.name: Enforce Pre-Start Quality Gates
step.description: Before any workflow logic executes, validate all pre_start quality gates. These are preconditions that MUST be true before the workflow can proceed. Examples: "SSD must be validated", "cost estimate must be approved", "character sheets must exist".
step.action: Read the `quality_gates.pre_start` array from workflow.yaml. For each gate: 1. Evaluate the `check` condition against the current context 2. If the check PASSES, continue to next gate 3. If the check FAILS, execute the `on_fail` action (typically HALT with message)

gate.on-pass: All pre-start gates passed. Proceeding to workflow execution.
gate.on-fail: HALT workflow execution. Display: "Pre-start gate failed: {gate.on_fail}" "Resolve this condition before running this workflow." If the gate failure suggests a prerequisite workflow (e.g., "SSD must be validated" implies running validate-ssd), suggest that workflow to the user.

step.name: Initialize Cost Tracking
step.description: Set up cost tracking for this workflow execution. Every API call that costs money must be logged to the actuals file.
step.action: Read `cost_tracking` from workflow.yaml. Resolve the `log_to` path for the actuals file. Load `pricing_source` (pricing.yaml) into context for cost calculations.

If the actuals file already exists (workflow is being resumed or re-run), load the existing running_total to continue tracking from where it left off. If it does not exist, initialize a new actuals tracking structure with: - episode_id - tracking_started: current timestamp - transactions: [] - running_total: { spent: 0.00, budget_status: "on-track" }
Load the cost estimate for this episode (if it exists) from _cost/estimates/. This provides the budget baseline for threshold alerts.

tag.name: step
tag.description: A numbered execution unit. Steps execute SEQUENTIALLY in order of their `n` attribute. Each step is a logical unit of work that may contain multiple actions, checks, and outputs.

attributes.attr: Step number. Determines execution order. Can be integer or string (e.g., "3a").
Human-readable step name for logging and progress display.
If "true", the step can be skipped without halting the workflow. In normal mode: ASK user whether to execute or skip. In yolo mode: Execute unless there is a reason to skip (missing inputs, etc.).

Conditional execution. The step ONLY executes if the condition evaluates to true. Conditions can reference: - Workflow context variables: if="episode_id exists" - Previous step outputs: if="step_3_output.status == 'success'" - Config values: if="resolution_profile == 'hd'" - File existence: if="file_exists('{episode_path}/ssd/scene-spec.yaml')" - Boolean flags: if="ssd_validated == true"

execution-rules.rule: Display step progress: "[Step {n}/{total}] {name}"
If the step has if="condition" and the condition is FALSE, skip with: "[Step {n} skipped: condition not met]"
If the step has optional="true" and execution is not possible, skip with: "[Step {n} skipped: optional, prerequisites not met]"
Each step's outputs are added to the workflow context for subsequent steps

tag.name: for-each
tag.description: Iterates over a collection, executing the contained block once per item. Used for processing multiple scenes, characters, formats, etc.

attributes.attr: The collection to iterate over. Can be: - A YAML array from the workflow context (e.g., "ssd.scenes") - A directory listing (e.g., "characters/*") - A computed list from a previous step
The loop variable name. Available inside the block as {as_name}. Example: as="scene" makes {scene.id}, {scene.duration}, etc. available.

execution-rules.rule: Before the loop, announce: "Processing {collection.length} items..."
For each iteration, set the loop variable in context
Display progress: "[{current}/{total}] Processing {item_identifier}..."
If an iteration fails and on_error is "continue", log the error and proceed to next item
If an iteration fails and on_error is "halt", stop the loop and halt the workflow
After the loop, summarize: "{success_count}/{total} items processed successfully"

tag.name: repeat
tag.description: Repeats the contained block a fixed number of times or until a condition is met. Used for retry logic, iterative refinement, and convergence loops.

attributes.attr: Fixed number of repetitions.
Condition that terminates the loop when TRUE. Evaluated after each iteration.
Safety limit — maximum iterations regardless of until condition. Prevents infinite loops.

execution-rules.rule: If both count and until are specified, stop at whichever comes first
If max iterations reached without until condition being met, WARN and exit loop
Track iteration count in context as {repeat.iteration}

tag.name: action
tag.description: A single executable instruction. The primary unit of work within a step. Claude Code reads the instruction and performs it.

attributes.attr: Conditional execution. Same condition syntax as step-level if. When present, this specific action only executes if the condition is true.
If "true", failure of this action halts the entire workflow. If "false" (default), failure is handled per on_error configuration.

execution-rules.rule: Execute the instruction text literally
Resolve any {variables} in the instruction before execution
If the action involves a tool/API call, apply the relevant loaded skill
If the action produces output, store it in the workflow context
If the action fails, route to the error handler based on error type

tag.name: check
tag.description: Evaluates a condition and routes to on-pass or on-fail handlers. Used for validation, assertions, and conditional branching within a step.

children.child: Actions to execute if the condition is TRUE.
Actions to execute if the condition is FALSE.

execution-rules.rule: Evaluate the condition against the current workflow context
Execute the appropriate branch (on-pass or on-fail)
If on-fail contains "HALT:", stop workflow execution immediately
If on-fail contains "WARN:", log warning but continue execution
If on-fail contains "ASK:", present the question to the user (respecting execution mode)

tag.name: ask
tag.description: Prompts the user for input. The response is stored in the workflow context under the specified key for use in subsequent steps.

attributes.attr: Variable name to store the user's response.
If "true" and the user does not provide input, re-ask (normal mode) or HALT (yolo mode).
Default value used when: - In yolo/yolo-uncapped mode (auto-selected) - User presses enter without typing (normal mode)
Comma-separated list of valid options. If provided, validate the user's response against these options.

execution-rules.rule: In NORMAL mode: Display the question and WAIT for user response
In YOLO mode: Use default if available. If required and no default, HALT with message.
In YOLO-UNCAPPED mode: Same as YOLO.
Store the response in context as {key}
If options are specified and user response is not in the list, re-ask with valid options shown

tag.name: goto
tag.description: Jumps execution to a different step. Use sparingly — sequential execution is preferred. Primarily used for error recovery branches and conditional skip-ahead logic.

attributes.attr: Target step number to jump to.
Condition that must be true for the jump to execute.

execution-rules.rule: Resolve the target step number
If the target step does not exist, HALT with error
Jump to the target step — do NOT re-execute steps between current and target
Log the jump: "[goto] Jumping from step {current} to step {target}: {reason}"

tag.name: invoke-workflow
tag.description: Invokes another workflow as a sub-workflow. The current workflow PAUSES, the sub-workflow executes to completion, and control returns to the invoking workflow. The sub-workflow's outputs are available in the parent workflow's context.

attributes.attr: Path to the workflow.yaml file to invoke. Supports {project-root} and other variables.
If "true", the current workflow context is passed to the sub-workflow. The sub-workflow can read (but not modify) the parent's context.
Variable name to store the sub-workflow's output in the parent context.

execution-rules.rule: Resolve the workflow path
Initialize the sub-workflow using the SAME engine (this file)
The sub-workflow inherits the current execution mode (normal/yolo/yolo-uncapped)
The sub-workflow has its OWN step counter (does not affect parent numbering)
When the sub-workflow completes, capture its outputs and continue parent execution
If the sub-workflow HALTs with an error, the parent workflow also HALTs
Log: "[invoke-workflow] Starting sub-workflow: {workflow.name}"
On completion: "[invoke-workflow] Sub-workflow {workflow.name} completed: {status}"

tag.name: invoke-task
tag.description: Invokes a standalone task from _cookie/core/tasks/. Tasks are simpler than workflows — they are single-purpose operations that do not have their own workflow.yaml. Tasks execute inline within the current step.

attributes.attr: Path to the task file (XML or MD).
Input data to pass to the task.
Variable name for the task's output.

execution-rules.rule: Load and execute the task file
Tasks execute within the current workflow's context (not a separate context)
Tasks inherit the current execution mode
Tasks can read the workflow context but their internal variables do not persist after completion
Log: "[invoke-task] Executing task: {task_name}"

tag.name: invoke-protocol
tag.description: Invokes a protocol from _cookie/core/engine/protocols/. Protocols are reusable sub-procedures that handle cross-cutting concerns: input discovery, preflight checks, handoff formatting, dependency resolution, and cost gating.

attributes.attr: Protocol name. Must be one of: - discover-inputs: Smart file discovery with FULL_LOAD, SELECTIVE_LOAD, INDEX_GUIDED strategies - preflight-check: Validate all variables and file references before step execution - handoff: Generate machine-readable workflow output for downstream consumption - dependency-resolve: Build and execute asset dependency graph from SSD - cost-gate: Estimate cost, present to user, require approval
Data to pass to the protocol.
Variable name for the protocol's output.

execution-rules.rule: Load the protocol XML from {protocols-path}/{name}.xml
Execute the protocol's instructions
Protocols can modify the workflow context (e.g., discover-inputs adds loaded files)
Protocol failures are handled per the workflow's on_error configuration
Log: "[protocol] Invoking {name}"

protocol.name: discover-inputs
protocol.behavior: For each input_file_pattern in the workflow config: 1. Resolve the path pattern 2. Detect load strategy (FULL_LOAD / SELECTIVE_LOAD / INDEX_GUIDED) 3. Handle ref: prefixed paths by resolving relative to {project-root} 4. FULL_LOAD: Read entire file content into context 5. SELECTIVE_LOAD: Read only specified keys/sections 6. INDEX_GUIDED: List directory contents, load individual files on demand 7. If file not found: trigger on_error.missing_file

protocol.name: preflight-check
protocol.behavior: Before executing a step or block: 1. Scan all {variable} references in the upcoming instructions 2. Verify each variable is resolved in the current context 3. Check that all referenced file paths exist on disk 4. Validate that required skills for the step are loaded 5. If any check fails: report all failures at once (batch, not one-by-one) 6. Attempt auto-resolution for common cases (e.g., {date} = today) 7. If unresolvable variables remain: trigger on_error.unresolved_variable

protocol.name: handoff
protocol.behavior: Generate a structured handoff document when a workflow completes: 1. Collect: workflow_id, status (success/partial/failed), completion timestamp 2. List all outputs produced (file paths, generated artifacts) 3. List any errors or warnings encountered 4. Include cost_summary (if cost tracking was active) 5. Suggest next_workflow based on the production pipeline position 6. Format as YAML for machine readability 7. This handoff is used by the Director agent to route to the next step

protocol.name: dependency-resolve
protocol.behavior: Build and resolve the asset dependency graph: 1. Load asset-dependency-graph.yaml from the episode SSD directory 2. Parse all nodes (assets) and edges (dependencies) 3. Topological sort to determine execution order 4. Identify which assets can be generated in PARALLEL (no mutual dependencies) 5. Identify which assets must be SEQUENTIAL (dependency chain) 6. Return an execution plan: ordered list of parallel batches 7. Example: Batch 1 = [character-refs, environment-refs] (parallel), Batch 2 = [first-frames] (depends on character-refs), Batch 3 = [video-clips] (depends on first-frames)

protocol.name: cost-gate
protocol.behavior: Estimate and gate costs before API spending: 1. Calculate the cost of the upcoming operation(s) using pricing.yaml 2. Look up the correct rate: tool -> model -> speed -> resolution -> per_unit_cost 3. Multiply by quantity (e.g., 8 seconds of video * $0.15/sec = $1.20) 4. Add retake buffer from global config (default 20%) 5. Check against thresholds: - Per-scene warn: $5.00, halt: $10.00 - Cumulative warn: 80% of estimate, halt: 120% of estimate 6. PRESENT the cost breakdown to the user 7. In NORMAL mode: REQUIRE explicit approval ("approved" / "yes" / "proceed") 8. In YOLO mode: REQUIRE approval (cost gates are still active in yolo) 9. In YOLO-UNCAPPED mode: Log the estimate and PROCEED without approval 10. If threshold exceeded: HALT regardless of mode and present options (reduce scope, change resolution profile, increase budget, abort) 11. Show resolution alternatives: "At draft profile: $X, at standard: $Y, at hd: $Z, at 4k: $W"

tag.name: template-output
tag.description: Presents formatted output to the user. This is the primary way workflows communicate results, summaries, previews, and deliverables to the user.

attributes.attr: If "true", this output MUST be displayed even in yolo mode. Use for final deliverables, important warnings, and error summaries. Critical outputs always pause for review in normal mode.

children.child: The template string with {variables} to be resolved and displayed. Supports markdown formatting for rich display.

An example of what the output looks like when rendered. Used as a reference for Claude Code to understand the expected format. The example is NOT displayed to the user — it is guidance for the engine.

execution-rules.rule: Resolve all {variables} in the format template
Display the rendered output to the user
In NORMAL mode: WAIT for user acknowledgment before proceeding
In NORMAL mode with critical="true": WAIT and explicitly ask for approval
In YOLO mode: Display and proceed immediately (no wait)
In YOLO mode with critical="true": Display and proceed, but add "[Critical output — review recommended]"
In YOLO-UNCAPPED mode: Same as YOLO

tag.name: parallel
tag.description: Declares a block of actions or sub-steps that can execute concurrently. Used for independent asset generation tasks that do not depend on each other. Example: Generating character reference images for multiple characters simultaneously, or generating narration audio while video clips are being produced.

attributes.attr: Maximum number of parallel tasks to run at once. Use to limit API rate or manage context window size.
- "all": All items must succeed for the block to succeed. - "best-effort": Continue even if some items fail; report failures at end.

execution-rules.rule: IMPORTANT: Claude Code is single-threaded. "Parallel" means the engine should identify independent tasks and batch them for efficient execution, NOT that they literally run simultaneously. The engine should: 1. Identify all independent tasks in the parallel block 2. Group them by tool/API (to batch API calls where possible) 3. Execute each group, proceeding to the next without waiting for user input between items (even in normal mode — confirmation happens at the parallel block level) 4. Track individual task results 5. Report aggregate results at the end of the parallel block
Cost tracking applies to EACH task individually within the parallel block
If strategy="all" and any task fails, the entire parallel block fails
If strategy="best-effort", failures are collected and reported at block end
Display progress: "[Parallel {current}/{total}] {task_description}"

tag.name: on-error
tag.description: Defines error handling behavior within a step or block. Overrides the workflow-level on_error configuration for the scope where it appears.

attributes.attr: The error type to handle. Must be one of: - missing_file: A required file was not found - unresolved_variable: A {variable} could not be resolved - validation_failure: A quality check or validation failed - api_failure: An external API call failed (VEO, Nano Banana, ElevenLabs) - cost_exceeded: Spending has exceeded a threshold - asset_quality_fail: Generated asset did not pass quality checks

handler.action: In NORMAL mode: Ask user to provide the file path, create the file, or skip. In YOLO mode: If the file is optional, skip with warning. If required, HALT.

HALT workflow execution immediately. Display all unresolved variables and where they are referenced. Suggest: Check config inheritance chain, verify episode-config.yaml exists, ensure all required context variables are set.
Retry the validation ONE time. If it fails again: In NORMAL mode: Present the failure and ASK user how to proceed (fix and retry, skip, abort). In YOLO mode: HALT with the validation failure details.

Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff: - Attempt 1: Wait 1 second, retry - Attempt 2: Wait 4 seconds, retry - Attempt 3: Wait 16 seconds, retry If all 3 retries fail: In NORMAL mode: Present the error and ASK user (retry again, skip this item, abort). In YOLO mode: Log the failure and continue to next item if possible, HALT if critical. ALWAYS log the failure (including all retry attempts) to the cost actuals file with status "failed" and cost 0.00 (failed calls are not billed).

HALT workflow execution immediately, regardless of execution mode. Display: 1. Current spending total 2. Original budget estimate 3. Which threshold was exceeded (per-scene or cumulative) 4. Breakdown of spending so far by tool 5. Remaining work and estimated cost to complete ASK user: "Increase budget? Reduce scope? Change resolution profile? Abort?" This handler fires even in YOLO-UNCAPPED mode at the hard halt threshold (120%).

Do NOT halt execution. Instead: 1. Flag the asset with status "flagged" in the asset tracking 2. Log the quality failure reason 3. Continue execution with the next asset/step 4. At the NEXT quality gate (per_scene or post_complete), present ALL flagged assets together for batch review In NORMAL mode: Present flagged assets at the next gate and ask for retake/accept/skip decisions. In YOLO mode: Auto-accept flagged assets unless they fail a critical quality criterion.

tag.name: gate
tag.description: A quality checkpoint that evaluates conditions and blocks or allows execution. Gates come in three types, corresponding to the quality_gates configuration: - pre_start: Checked during INIT-6 before any workflow logic - per_scene: Checked after each scene is processed - post_complete: Checked after all workflow steps complete, before handoff

attributes.attr: Gate type: pre_start, per_scene, post_complete.

execution-rules.rule: Load the relevant quality_gates array from workflow.yaml for this gate type. Evaluate each check condition. Collect all results before acting.
If ALL checks pass: Display "[Gate: {type}] All checks passed" and continue.

If any check FAILS: - If on_fail starts with "HALT:": Stop execution, display the message. - If on_fail starts with "WARN:": Log warning, display to user, continue. - If on_fail starts with "ASK:": Present the question (respecting execution mode).
Per-scene gates also check for flagged assets from asset_quality_fail errors. Present all flagged assets for review at this point.

tag.name: cost-gate
tag.description: A specialized gate that estimates the cost of upcoming work and requires approval. This is a convenience tag that invokes the cost-gate protocol.

attributes.attr: What to estimate: "step" (current step only), "scene" (current scene), "remaining" (all remaining work), "batch" (current parallel batch).

execution-rules.rule: Invoke the cost-gate protocol with the specified scope
Behavior depends on execution mode (see protocol definition above)
If the cost gate is rejected by the user, HALT with cost summary
If approved, log the approval timestamp and proceed

step-execution-runtime.description: This section defines the runtime behavior for executing workflow steps. After initialization (Section 3), the engine reads the instructions.xml file from the workflow's installed_path and processes its steps using these rules.

execution-loop.rule: Steps execute in order of their `n` attribute. The engine MUST NOT skip ahead or execute steps out of order unless explicitly directed by a goto tag.

For each step, the engine follows this lifecycle: 1. CHECK the step's `if` condition (if present). Skip if false. 2. CHECK if the step is optional and its prerequisites are not met. Skip if so. 3. ANNOUNCE the step: "[Step {n}/{total}] {name}" 4. RUN preflight-check on the step's contents (resolve variables, verify files) 5. EXECUTE all actions, checks, and sub-blocks within the step IN ORDER 6. HANDLE any errors per the step's on-error or the workflow's on_error config 7. STORE the step's outputs in the workflow context 8. EVALUATE any per_scene quality gates (if this step processes a scene) 9. LOG the step completion: "[Step {n} complete] {summary}"

The workflow context is a key-value store that accumulates data as steps execute. Each step can read from and write to the context. Context persists across steps within a single workflow execution. Sub-workflows receive a COPY of the parent context and return their outputs — they cannot modify the parent context directly.

When resolving a {variable}, check these sources in order (first match wins): 1. Step-local variables (set within the current step) 2. Workflow context (accumulated from previous steps) 3. Workflow.yaml fields 4. Episode config overrides 5. Module config 6. Global config (global.yaml) 7. System values (date, project-root, etc.) If not found in any source: trigger on_error.unresolved_variable

For actions that produce files (asset generation, document creation, exports): SAVE the output file IMMEDIATELY after the action completes. Do NOT batch file saves to the end of a step. This ensures that if a later action fails, the earlier work is preserved.

cost-tracking-runtime.description: Every API call that incurs a cost MUST be tracked. This section defines the cost logging protocol that runs alongside step execution.

on-api-call.description: This block executes AROUND every API call to a paid service (VEO, Nano Banana, ElevenLabs). It runs automatically — workflow authors do not need to add explicit cost tracking.

before-call.action: Calculate the expected cost from pricing.yaml

Check cumulative spending against thresholds: - If cumulative + expected > warn_threshold (80% of estimate): WARN - If cumulative + expected > halt_threshold (120% of estimate): trigger cost_exceeded error
Check per-scene spending against thresholds: - If scene_cost + expected > per_scene_warn ($5.00): WARN - If scene_cost + expected > per_scene_halt ($10.00): trigger cost_exceeded error

after-call.action: Log the transaction to the actuals file: { id: "TXN-{sequential_id}", timestamp: "{ISO 8601 timestamp}", scene_id: "{current_scene_id}", tool: "{tool_name}", operation: "{operation_type}", model: "{model_used}", speed: "{speed_setting}", resolution: "{resolution_used}", duration: {duration_if_applicable}, cost: {calculated_cost}, status: "success", output: "{output_file_path}", is_retake: {true/false}, retake_number: {N if retake}, retake_reason: "{reason if retake}" }
Recalculate running_total.estimated_remaining based on remaining work items

Update running_total.budget_status: - "on-track" if spent is at or below the proportional estimate - "over-budget" if spent exceeds the proportional estimate - "critical" if spent exceeds 80% of total estimate
Save the updated actuals file to disk IMMEDIATELY
Display compact cost update (always, in all modes): "[$X.XX] {tool}: {operation} ({scene_id}) — Total: ${running_total}"
Log the failed transaction with cost: 0.00 and status: "failed". Include the error message for debugging.
Route to the api_failure error handler

retake-tracking.description: When an asset is regenerated (retake), track it separately from first-time generations. Retakes count toward the retake budget.

rules.rule: Mark retake transactions with is_retake: true and increment retake_number
Include retake_reason (e.g., "character inconsistency", "wrong camera angle")
Track retake count per scene against max_retakes_per_scene from global config

When max retakes reached for a scene: In NORMAL mode: ASK "Scene {scene_id} has used {count}/{max} retakes (${cost} spent). Continue with another retake, accept current version, or skip this scene?" In YOLO mode: Accept the current version and flag for review. In YOLO-UNCAPPED mode: Continue with one more retake, then accept if still failing.

quality-gate-enforcement.description: Quality gates are checkpoints that validate work products at defined points in the workflow. They are defined in the workflow.yaml under quality_gates and enforced by this engine at three stages.

gate-stage.name: pre_start
gate-stage.when: During initialization (INIT-6), before any workflow steps execute.
gate-stage.behavior: Evaluate ALL pre_start checks. If ANY fail with a HALT action, the workflow does not start. Collect all failures and present them together — do not fail on the first check and hide the rest.
gate-stage.on-fail: Display all failed pre_start gates with their on_fail messages. Suggest prerequisite workflows that would satisfy the failed conditions. Example: "Pre-start gate failed: SSD must be validated. Run /cookie-validate-ssd first."

gate-stage.name: per_scene
gate-stage.when: After each scene is processed within a for-each loop over scenes. Also triggered at the end of each iteration in a parallel block processing scenes.
gate-stage.behavior: Evaluate per_scene checks for the current scene. Also present any assets flagged by asset_quality_fail during this scene's processing. In NORMAL mode: Present results and wait for user decision on flagged assets. In YOLO mode: Auto-accept unless a critical criterion fails.
gate-stage.on-fail: For WARN actions: Log and continue. For HALT actions: Stop processing further scenes. Present failure details. For flagged assets: Present the list and ask for retake/accept/skip decisions.

gate-stage.name: post_complete
gate-stage.when: After all workflow steps have completed, before generating the handoff.
gate-stage.behavior: Final validation of the workflow's outputs. This is the last chance to catch issues before the work is handed off to the next workflow in the pipeline. Evaluate ALL post_complete checks.
gate-stage.on-fail: Display all failures. In NORMAL mode: Ask user whether to accept with warnings, retry failed items, or abort. In YOLO mode: If all failures are WARNs, accept. If any are HALTs, stop and report.

gate.action: Evaluate all post_complete quality checks from workflow.yaml

step.name: Generate Cost Summary
step.action: Calculate the final cost summary for this workflow execution: - Total spent during this execution - Comparison to estimate (if estimate existed) - Breakdown by tool - Retake summary (count, cost, reasons) - Budget status

invoke-protocol.name: handoff

step.name: Update Production State

action.if: final_status == 'success' AND workflow.production_state_flags is defined AND episode_id exists in context

step.action:
Update the episode's production state flags and optionally transition episode status.
Procedure: 1. READ {episode_path}/episode-config.yaml 2. ENSURE the `production_state` block exists. If missing, create it with all 13 flags set to false: script_finalized, characters_created, style_guide_created, ssd_created, ssd_validated, cost_approved, assets_generated, asset_qa_passed, composition_assembled, preview_approved, final_rendered, exports_complete, final_qa_passed 3. SET each flag declared in workflow.production_state_flags to true 4. EVALUATE status auto-transitions (forward-only): Status ordinals: not-started(0), planning(1), pre-production(2), in-production(3), post-production(4), complete(5) Only transition if new_ordinal > current_ordinal: - script_finalized → "pre-production" (ordinal 2) - assets_generated → "in-production" (ordinal 3) - composition_assembled → "post-production" (ordinal 4) - final_qa_passed → "complete" (ordinal 5) 5. UPDATE metadata.last_modified to current date 6. WRITE updated YAML back to disk 7. LOG: "Production state updated: flags={flags_set}, status={old_status} → {new_status}"

step.name: Update Agent Memory
step.action: Check if the executing agent has a memory sidecar in _cookie/_memory/. If so, append relevant decisions, learnings, and notes to the sidecar. What to remember: - Prompt patterns that produced good/bad results - Configuration choices that worked well - Unexpected issues and how they were resolved - User preferences expressed during this workflow Do NOT store: raw file contents, full API responses, cost transaction details (those are in _cost/actuals/).

pattern.name: asset-generation-pipeline
pattern.description: The standard pattern for workflows that generate assets (video clips, images, audio). This pattern combines dependency resolution, parallel execution, cost gating, and quality enforcement.

cost-gate.action: Calculate total cost for all assets to be generated
Present breakdown by scene and tool

for-each.action: Generate {asset} using the appropriate skill and tool

gate.action: Evaluate quality checks for all assets in this batch
Verify all required assets were generated

pattern.name: document-generation
pattern.description: Pattern for workflows that produce documents (scripts, SSDs, character sheets). These workflows typically involve iterative refinement with user feedback.

step.name: Generate Draft
step.action: Generate the initial document draft

step.name: User Review
step.ask: Review the draft above. Provide feedback or say "approved" to proceed.

step.name: Save and Validate
step.action: Save the approved document

invoke-task.path: validate task for this document type

pattern.name: multi-format-export
pattern.description: Pattern for exporting a single production to multiple format targets (16:9, 9:16, 1:1, etc.) from one SSD. Uses the format adaptation engine.

step.name: Identify Export Formats
step.action: Read format_exports from episode-config.yaml. For each target format, load the corresponding format preset.

step.name: Render {format}
step.action: Render the adapted SSD to the target format
Save to {episode_path}/exports/

pattern.name: scene-continuity
pattern.description: Pattern for ensuring visual continuity between scenes using the first-frame/last-frame approach. This is COOKIE's primary continuity mechanism, chosen over VEO's extension API because it works at any resolution and gives explicit visual control.

step.name: Generate First Frame
step.action: For the current scene, generate a first-frame image using Nano Banana. The first frame must visually match the last frame of the PREVIOUS scene (if this is not the first scene). Inject character reference images and environment references into the prompt.

step.name: Generate Video Clip
step.action: Generate the video clip using VEO with the first frame as the starting image. Use image-to-video mode for continuity.

step.name: Extract Last Frame
step.action: Extract the last frame from the generated video clip using FFmpeg. Save as last-frame.png in the scene's assets directory. This last frame becomes the continuity reference for the NEXT scene.

on-pass.action: Compare the first frame of this scene with the last frame of the previous scene. Check for visual consistency: character appearance, lighting, environment.

engine-directives.directive:
These rules apply at ALL times during workflow execution, regardless of mode or context.
SAVE outputs after EACH action that produces a file. Never batch saves. LOG every API call cost to _cost/actuals/. No exceptions. TRACK execution mode and respect mode-specific rules at all decision points. PRESERVE existing files unless explicitly instructed to overwrite. Use versioning (v1, v2, v3) for regenerated assets. DISPLAY progress indicators for every step, loop iteration, and parallel batch. RESOLVE all {variables} before executing any action that uses them. RESPECT the config inheritance chain: global -> module -> episode. Never use a global value when an episode override exists. ENFORCE quality gates. Quality gates cannot be skipped in ANY execution mode. HALT on unresolved critical variables. Never guess or fabricate values. WRITE cost actuals to disk immediately after each transaction. Never hold cost data only in memory.

These actions are PROHIBITED under all circumstances.
NEVER execute an API call without checking cost thresholds first. NEVER skip a quality gate, even in yolo-uncapped mode. NEVER overwrite an asset file without versioning. If video-v1.mp4 exists and a retake is needed, create video-v2.mp4. NEVER proceed past a HALT error. HALT means STOP. NEVER fabricate or hallucinate file paths, costs, or API responses. NEVER load ALL skill supplementary files at once. Load JIT as needed during execution. NEVER modify the global.yaml, pricing.yaml, or module config.yaml files during workflow execution. These are configuration-time files, not runtime files. NEVER execute steps out of order unless directed by a goto tag. NEVER discard cost tracking data. Even if a workflow fails, the actuals file must reflect all spending that occurred. NEVER auto-approve a cost gate in normal or yolo mode. Only yolo-uncapped skips cost approval.

These are best practices that should be followed when possible.
PREFER loading skill supplementary files on-demand over pre-loading everything. PREFER parallel execution over sequential when the dependency graph allows it. PREFER presenting batch failures together over one-at-a-time error dialogs. PREFER the standard resolution profile unless the user or episode config specifies otherwise. PREFER retrying with a modified prompt over retrying with the exact same prompt on asset_quality_fail. PREFER compact progress output in yolo mode and detailed output in normal mode. PREFER showing cost impact whenever a user decision affects spending (e.g., choosing hd over standard). PREFER using the episode's existing assets when available rather than regenerating them.

quick-reference.title: COOKIE Workflow Engine — Execution Quick Reference
quick-reference.lifecycle: 1. DETECT project root (find _cookie/ directory) 2. PARSE workflow.yaml (the "what") 3. RESOLVE config inheritance (global -> module -> episode) 4. LOAD skills JIT (tool knowledge files) 5. DISCOVER inputs (files the workflow needs) 6. ENFORCE pre_start quality gates 7. INITIALIZE cost tracking 8. ANNOUNCE workflow start 9. EXECUTE steps sequentially (the "how" — defined in instructions.xml) - For each step: check conditions -> preflight -> execute -> handle errors -> store outputs - Respect execution mode at every decision point - Track costs for every API call - Enforce per_scene quality gates 10. ENFORCE post_complete quality gates 11. GENERATE cost summary 12. GENERATE handoff document 13. UPDATE production state (if workflow declares production_state_flags) 14. UPDATE agent memory (if applicable) 15. PRESENT completion summary
